topic: Reading a Biography: Commander Abhilash Tomy
title: Commander Abhilash Tomy's Incredible Journey and Parenthesis
explanation_chunks: This page tells the amazing true story of Commander Abhilash Tomy, an Indian Naval officer who did something truly extraordinary. In 2013, he became the first Indian person to sail all the way around the world by himself, without stopping!

Later, in 2018, he joined another big sailing race called the Golden Globe Race. It was a super tough challenge, and he faced a terrible storm that badly damaged his boat. He got hurt and couldn't move easily.

Luckily, after four days adrift, rescue teams found him. He was taken to an island for help. His bravery and strong will helped him survive this incredibly difficult journey, showing how determined he is!

We also learn about a special way to add extra information to our sentences using brackets, called parenthesis. This extra detail makes the sentence more interesting, but the sentence would still make sense without it.
examples: Commander Tomy (who was only allowed to use paper maps) had a very tough journey.
My favourite animal (a fluffy cat) loves to nap in the sun.
key_points: Commander Abhilash Tomy completed an amazing solo journey around the world.
A biography tells the factual story of a real person's life, written by someone else.
Brackets are used for parenthesis, which adds extra, non-essential information to a sentence.

vocabulary.definition: travel all the way around something, like the Earth.
an exact copy of something, like a model boat.
unable to move, often because of an injury.
a non-fiction story about someone else's life.
extra information added to a sentence, usually within brackets, dashes, or commas.

explain_again: This page tells us about a super brave sailor who sailed all around the world by himself! It was really hard, and he got hurt in a storm, but he was rescued. We also learned how to use special little curvy lines called brackets to tuck in extra details into our sentences without confusing anyone!
fun_analogy: Commander Tomy's journey was like a giant game of 'follow the leader' around the world, but the leader was the sun and stars! Brackets are like secret whispers in a sentence; they add fun facts but aren't strictly necessary.
